refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to ensure packet delivery.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication adaptation and teleoperation control responsiveness.
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality control, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of insufficient adaptation speed leading to temporary packet loss spikes.
Risk that error correction overhead may impact latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring (DP3) as a complementary DP to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality metrics to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not focused on communication quality control.
Physical vehicle components have limited direct control over packet loss.
Primary module responsible for teleoperation communication; best containment for DP.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not control communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality control, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Enhance interface definitions with Safety Services to ensure timely safety alerts related to communication degradation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear performance targets and measurable parameters.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is well contained within Teleoperation module.
Need to monitor interface stability with Network Link.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network packet analysis within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface protocols with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.
Consider adding complementary DP for real-time monitoring to improve robustness.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a suitable DP, and assigns it to an appropriate IM with clear interfaces and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs for further detail.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module has capability to implement adaptive bitrate and error correction.
open_risks: Potential latency impact from error correction overhead.
Dependency on stable Network Link interface.
evidence_needed: Performance data from adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms under representative network conditions.
Interface stability and packet loss metrics from Teleoperation module.
